meaning: adj.[生]外生的,[地]外成的,[医]外因的
examples: These rivers and river systems are known as "exogenous" because their sources lie outside the arid zone.
Exogenous quinolinic acid is endowed with quite evident anti-inflammatory effects on either acute or subacute models.
Exogenous variables can include lagged values of endogenous variables.
This exogenous event has actually forced Japan into having one of the loosest monetary policies globally.
With exogenous red-lights flashing brightly in the euro zone, demand for the dollar is building.
